title: Routing queue policies diverge: forward-buffer eviction/dequeue/expiry, pending-discovery bounds, DTN buffer model
description: Forward buffer (constants agree 8 sources x 2 packets): eviction when a new source arrives at capacity - Python evicts entire LRU source's packets (router.py:208-219), C evicts entire oldest source slot (router.c:809-820), Rust evicts only the single globally-oldest packet (forward_buffer.rs:126-131). Dequeue order: Python per-source FIFO; Rust global (priority, then oldest); C global oldest-first, no priority. Expiry edge: Python drops at deadline <= now; Rust only strictly past deadline; C fixed per-packet age vs constant. Pending-discovery: C has global cap MAX_PENDING=8 with global oldest-eviction plus per-dest 3 (router.c:271-328); Python (router.py:532-540) and Rust (hybrid.rs:301-313) bound per-dest only - total unbounded; expiry at age >= timeout (Py/Rust) vs > timeout (C). DTN buffer: Python/Rust byte-bounded with evict-to-fit and reject-expired-at-insert (router.py:645-682, routing.rs:508-541); C slot-count bounded, no insert-time expiry, disabled by default (router.c:623-752, Kconfig default 0). Same offered load => different retention/drop behavior per implementation. Define one policy set in spec 05-routing and align.
